description_long: This is a tall, slender ceramic vase with a narrow neck, flared mouth, and a single curved handle attached from the neck to the shoulder. The body is decorated with intricate black-figure artwork, depicting human and possibly mythological figures in dynamic poses, surrounded by stylized patterns and motifs. The upper shoulder features a band of repeated leaf or petal designs, while the lower body transitions to a solid black base. The surface shows some signs of wear, with areas of faded paint and minor abrasions, but the decorative details remain largely intact. The vase stands on a small, circular foot that provides stability. Its coloration is primarily a warm terracotta with black painted details, typical of ancient Greek pottery.
description: A tall, slender terracotta vase with a flared mouth, single handle, and black-figure artwork depicting human figures and decorative motifs. The surface is worn but retains intricate painted details.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main decorative scene with human figures and ornate patterns, as well as the handle on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the plain terracotta surface with minimal decoration and the full curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: Side view displays the handle's attachment and a partial view of the painted figures and motifs.
description_view_3: Opposite front view reveals additional decorative motifs and the continuation of the black-figure artwork.
